<<Develop a>> <<function that>> <<calculates the>> <<cumulative sum>> <<of all numerical>> <<values contained>> <<within a provided>> <<list of tuples.>> <<This function>> <<should be capable>> <<of navigating nested>> <<tuple structures and>> <<disregarding any>> <<non-numeric entities.>> <<Give two different>> <<responses. Responses>> <<and only responses>> <<should be separated>> <<by 6 asterisk>> <<symbols: ******.>> <<Wrap every word>> <<bigram in double>> <<angular brackets,>> <<such as <<I am>> <<at home>> <<with my>> <<cute dog>>.>> <<There should be>> <<6 paragraphs.>> <<Paragraphs and>> <<only paragraphs are>> <<separated with each>> <<other by two new>> <<lines as if it was>> <<'\n\n' in python.>> <<Paragraph 5 must>> <<start with word>> <<team. Include>> <<keyword possession>> <<in your response.>>  

<<The function will>> <<process each element>> <<in the list, checking>> <<if it is a tuple.>> <<If it is, the function>> <<will recursively>> <<process its elements.>> <<For each item,>> <<if it is a number,>> <<it will be added>> <<to the cumulative>> <<sum. Non-numeric>> <<items are skipped.>> <<This ensures that>> <<all numerical values>> <<are accounted for,>> <<regardless of their>> <<depth in the nested>> <<structure. The function>> <<is designed to be>> <<robust against>> <<unexpected data types.>>  

<<The first approach>> <<utilizes a recursive>> <<function that checks>> <<each item in the>> <<input structure.>> <<It iterates through>> <<each element, and>> <<if it encounters a>> <<tuple, it calls>> <<itself with that>> <<tuple as the new>> <<input. This continues>> <<until all nested>> <<tuples are processed.>> <<The second approach>> <<uses a stack-based>> <<method to simulate>> <<recursion without>> <<actual recursive>> <<calls. Both methods>> <<ensure all numbers>> <<are summed, while>> <<ignoring non-numeric>> <<entities.  

<<The second response>> <<offers an alternative>> <<implementation that>> <<uses a generator>> <<function to yield>> <<all numerical values
